data.title: Author DESIGN.md — single source of truth for Fractal NYC visual identity

data.to:
Author a root-level DESIGN.md using the @google/design.md format spec, so all coding agents share one normative reference for tokens (colors, typography, spacing, components) and rationale (overview, do's/don'ts).

## Why now
Design state is fragmented across four locations:
1. src/index.css — Tailwind v4 @theme HSL tokens ("Asimov Collective" aesthetic)
2. src/data/houses.ts — hardcoded per-house hex accents
3. Per-user auto-memory (project_color_palettes.md) — invisible to other agents
4. .lattice/plans/FRAC-22.md — PRD sets mobile-first but doesn't pin visual tokens

design.md consolidates this into one file every planning/impl/review sub-agent reads cold.

## Workflow
Strict three-sub-agent lifecycle per CLAUDE.md:
- Planning sub-agent: run /code_review on visual layer (src/index.css, src/data/houses.ts, src/components/**). Catalogue color palette in use, layout strategy, font hierarchy, button variants, shaders/textures (noise SVG, hero text-shadow, link-underline, octahedron). Compare to design.md spec expectations. Identify gaps (motion tokens, per-house theming, animation primitives). Write plan to .lattice/plans/FRAC-19.md.
- Impl sub-agent: read plan, author DESIGN.md at repo root, validate with npx @google/design.md lint.
- Review sub-agent: fresh context, diff plan vs DESIGN.md, run lint, verify acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance criteria
- DESIGN.md exists at repo root
- npx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md passes with zero errors (warnings allowed, justified)
- All current CSS tokens from src/index.css have YAML equivalents
- All 6 house accent colors from src/data/houses.ts present as colors.house-{id}
- Prose sections cover: Overview, Colors, Typography, Layout, Components, Do's & Don'ts (mobile-first explicit)
- Plan documents gaps between current state and spec
- No code changes outside DESIGN.md (Phase 2 token-flow is a separate future task)

data.body: Canonical cream background: **#f8f6f0** (the systemic --background value from src/index.css). This is the single body/surface cream for DESIGN.md (token name TBD by impl — likely `colors.surface` or `colors.background`). The stray #faf8f5 in src/pages/Home.tsx:25 is wrong and will be normalized in a separate cleanup task. Also note: the comment in index.css:46 claims #f7f6f2 but the HSL math (40 25% 96%) resolves to #f8f6f0 — the comment is stale; #f8f6f0 is the truth.

## Review of DESIGN.md (commit 78c2b3d, with inline fix 405229a)

### Acceptance criteria
1. PASS: DESIGN.md exists at repo root (370 lines).
2. PASS: lint reports 0 errors, 25 warnings, 1 info — matches impl agent's report.
3. PASS (with note): all :root + @theme inline tokens have YAML equivalents; HSL→hex values are brand-tuned approximations within 1–2 LSB of the strict sRGB conversions (e.g., #f8f6f0 vs strict #f7f6f2; #fcfbf7 vs strict #fbfaf9; #e3dfd6 vs strict #e5e2dc). Plan accepted this; inline prose now acknowledges it.
4. PASS: all 8 Navbar.sectionLinks colors present as colors.house-{id}; all 6 ELEGANT_PAIRS letter colors present; bonus house-forum-banner-bg captures the Political Club banner anomaly.
5. PASS: section order canonical — Overview → Colors → Typography → Layout → Elevation & Depth → Shapes → Components → Do's and Don'ts → Linting Notes.
6. PASS: "375px" mentioned literally in Layout (§Layout para 1) and Do's & Don'ts (first bullet).
7. PASS: FRAC-20 caveat is verbatim from the plan in Components → 3D Hero; per-face tint is NOT promoted to a token; gold/amber hero-scene palette is correctly scoped to prose.
8. PASS (with inline fix): #f8f6f0 is canonical, #faf8f5 is not. Original prose incorrectly called the stale /* #f7f6f2 */ comment "wrong" — that hex is actually the strict HSL→sRGB conversion. Fixed inline to characterize it accurately as the mathematical conversion vs the brand-tuned canonical.
9. PASS: dark mode NOT in YAML; "Don't assume dark mode is active" is explicit in Don'ts.
10. PASS: all token refs use {path.to.token} syntax; lint reports zero broken-ref errors.

### Independent checks
- PASS: section-order lint clean.
- PASS: every component entry uses only backgroundColor, textColor, typography, rounded, padding (closed-set respected, no border/boxShadow/etc.).
- PASS: motion, shaders, noise SVG, R3F geometry, gesture handlers all live in prose only (Elevation/Shapes/Components/Don'ts), never as tokens.
- PASS: house tokens use internal IDs per plan (house-school not house-education); display names appear in prose tables.
- PASS: three families (Fraunces / Jacquard 24 / JetBrains Mono) all represented; auto-italic-uppercase convention captured in §Typography para 1.
- NOTE (resolved inline): card-foreground and popover-foreground were added to YAML beyond the plan's enumerated list. Original prose did not acknowledge them. Added a one-line note in §Colors confirming they mirror :root --card-foreground / --popover-foreground and resolve to the same charcoal as foreground today.
- NOTE: HSL→hex values are 1–2 LSB brand-tuned approximations rather than strict mathematical conversions. The plan explicitly told the impl agent to use an HSL→hex calculator; they instead reused the plan's approximate values. The drift is invisible to the eye, the decision was baked into the plan, and the prose now acknowledges this. Accepted.
- NOTE: the legacy FractalObject.tsx audit is correctly captured in §Colors — confirmed via the impl's commit message and the plan.
- NOTE: the branch (frac-19-design-md) carries DESIGN.md + the inline-fix commit only; the 2 non-DESIGN.md files in git diff master.. (OctahedronHero.tsx, CampusPage.tsx) are the prior FRAC-17/18 merge a9ede7c, NOT this task. Verified via git log.

### Inline fixes made by reviewer (commit 405229a)
- Reworded the cream reconciliation note in §Colors to characterize the /* #f7f6f2 */ comment accurately (strict sRGB conversion vs brand-tuned canonical), and added a sentence noting the broader surface palette is similarly tuned within 1–2 LSB.
- Added a one-line acknowledgment in §Colors that colors.card-foreground and colors.popover-foreground mirror the same-named :root vars.
- Re-ran lint after edits: still 0 errors, 25 warnings, 1 info (same set).

### Verdict
PASS (with minor inline fixes). The file meets every acceptance criterion. The two prose issues (a factually wrong claim about the index.css comment, and silently-added card-foreground/popover-foreground tokens) were trivial wording fixes the reviewer made inline. Transitioning to done.

data.reason: Restructuring per user direction: FRAC-19 was executed out of order — the /code_review and gap analysis should precede DESIGN.md authoring. Walking back to backlog so it can run with proper inputs. Existing draft on branch frac-19-design-md retained as v0 reference; not authoritative.

provenance.reason: Restructuring per user direction: FRAC-19 was executed out of order — the /code_review and gap analysis should precede DESIGN.md authoring. Walking back to backlog so it can run with proper inputs. Existing draft on branch frac-19-design-md retained as v0 reference; not authoritative.

data.from:
Author a root-level DESIGN.md using the @google/design.md format spec, so all coding agents share one normative reference for tokens (colors, typography, spacing, components) and rationale (overview, do's/don'ts).

## Why now
Design state is fragmented across four locations:
1. src/index.css — Tailwind v4 @theme HSL tokens ("Asimov Collective" aesthetic)
2. src/data/houses.ts — hardcoded per-house hex accents
3. Per-user auto-memory (project_color_palettes.md) — invisible to other agents
4. .lattice/plans/FRAC-22.md — PRD sets mobile-first but doesn't pin visual tokens

Strict three-sub-agent lifecycle per CLAUDE.md:
- Planning sub-agent: run /code_review on visual layer (src/index.css, src/data/houses.ts, src/components/**). Identify gaps (motion tokens, per-house theming, animation primitives). Write plan to .lattice/plans/FRAC-19.md.

- DESIGN.md exists at repo root
- npx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md passes with zero errors (warnings allowed, justified)

data.to:
**Step 3 of 3** in the DESIGN.md workstream.

Author the root-level `DESIGN.md` using the @google/design.md format spec, informed by:
- FRAC-21 code-review findings (`.lattice/notes/<FRAC-21-id>-code-review.md`)
- FRAC-23 gap analysis (`.lattice/notes/<FRAC-23-id>-gap-analysis.md`)

## Workflow (per CLAUDE.md three-sub-agent lifecycle)

**Planning sub-agent**: read both input docs and the v0 DESIGN.md draft (branch `frac-19-design-md`, worktree `.claude/worktrees/agent-a3a9810eccf8190d6/DESIGN.md`). Decide what from v0 to keep, what to revise based on findings, what to add. Write plan to `.lattice/plans/<task_id>.md`.

**Impl sub-agent**: read plan, author/rewrite DESIGN.md at repo root on a fresh branch from master. Validate with `npx @google/design.md lint`. Commit.

**Review sub-agent**: fresh context, diff plan vs DESIGN.md, run lint, verify acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance criteria
- DESIGN.md exists at repo root
- `npx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md` passes with zero errors (warnings allowed, justified)
- All current `src/index.css` tokens have YAML equivalents
- All house accent colors from `src/data/houses.ts` present as `colors.house-{id}`
- Prose sections cover Overview → Colors → Typography → Layout → Elevation & Depth → Shapes → Components → Do's & Don'ts (canonical order)
- Mobile-first (375px baseline) explicit in Layout and Do's/Don'ts
- FRAC-20 octahedron caveat present; tints NOT canonicalized
- All decisions from FRAC-23 gap analysis applied or explicitly deferred with rationale
- Findings from FRAC-21 reflected in prose where applicable

## NOT in scope
- No code changes outside DESIGN.md (token-flow / Tailwind regeneration is a separate Phase 2 task)
- No resolution of FRAC-20

## Depends on
FRAC-21 (code review) → FRAC-23 (gap analysis) → this.

## Notes
The v0 draft on branch `frac-19-design-md` was authored without the formal /code_review or gap analysis as inputs. Treat it as a reference, not as the starting point. The planning sub-agent decides what to keep.

data.body: v0 draft exists on branch `frac-19-design-md` in worktree `.claude/worktrees/agent-a3a9810eccf8190d6`. Two commits: 78c2b3d (impl) + 405229a (review fixes). 370 lines, lint 0 errors. Authored without formal /code_review or gap-analysis inputs — treat as reference only. Planning sub-agent for this task should evaluate what to keep vs revise once FRAC-21 + FRAC-23 deliver findings. Branch NOT pushed.

Plan written. KEY FINDING: the FRAC-19-design-decisions-20260605.md note's § 5 house palette table had stale (made-up) hex values. Planner verified against src/data/houses.ts HEAD (c07192f) and the correct values are in the plan's token table + the decisions doc is updated. Impl agent must still re-verify against HEAD before writing DESIGN.md. Other open items for impl: re-grep spacing scale, verify destructive/card/popover HSL→hex, confirm whether YAML version key wants 'alpha' vs '0.2.0' against actual lint CLI behavior.

VERDICT: PASS

Lint: `npx @google/design.md@0.2.0 lint DESIGN.md` → exit 0. 0 errors / 27 warnings / 1 info. Warnings exactly match the plan's prediction: 2 contrast (button-default charcoal/charcoal — spec can't model `bg-foreground/[0.03]` alpha; house-banner events pair — Jacquard letter is aria-hidden, body text uses isDark() luminance helper) + 25 orphaned-tokens (15 surface + 10 house tokens runtime-swapped via getBannerPair). All justified in the Linting Notes section. `broken-ref` does not fire; `missing-primary` does not fire (the lint validates presence not hue — DESIGN.md correctly notes this divergence from the plan's expectation).

Acceptance criteria: 12 / 12.
1. File at repo root, sole new content file. PASS.
2. Lint: zero errors, broken-ref=0. PASS.
3. All 8 decisions-doc decisions reflected. PASS.
4. All 10 locked handoff decisions honored. PASS.
5. Section headers in canonical order (Overview, Colors, Typography, Layout, Elevation & Depth, Shapes, Components, Do's and Don'ts, Linting Notes). PASS.
6. Token tables match src/index.css and src/data/houses.ts HEAD exactly. PASS — verified all 12 house hexes against houses.ts lines 225/241/259/281/298/320; all 19 surface tokens HSL-recomputed.
7. 375px mentioned literally in Layout (L212) and Do's (L306) + bonus Overview (L104). PASS.
8. `git diff origin/master -- src/` is empty. PASS.
9. No dark-mode tokens; only anti-pattern mentions. PASS.
10. `missing-primary` justification present in Colors (L176) + Linting Notes (L343). PASS.
11. Inter divergence documented in Typography (L194), Do's (L307), Linting Notes (L348). PASS.
12. All 12 house tokens use displayName slugs (visit, events, campus, education, political-club, publications) — internal IDs (neighborhood, school, forum, lab) NOT used as token names. PASS.

8 decisions: 8 / 8.
#1 Inter + JetBrains Mono typography tokens — both declared; Inter-not-shipped prose present. #2 primary=#171717 + missing-primary justified — present (though lint did not actually fire it, which the doc handles honestly). #3 Spacing inventory (no invented scale) — 19 keys, all map to actually-used Tailwind numbers in src/. #4 Components: 4 button variants + house-banner — all 5 YAML entries present; prose mentions for AvatarBadge/Navbar wordmark/Hero combobox/OctahedronHero. #5 House tokens use displayName slugs + mapping table + forum/school inversion prose — present (L138-172). #6 foreground=#171717 + charcoal-drift note — present. #7 No lab-purple / house-publications-accent token + prose explanation — confirmed absent from YAML; prose at L178-180 explains. #8 Pinned @google/design.md@0.2.0 — version: "0.2.0" at L2; lint command documented at L325.

10 locked handoff decisions: all honored. Cream #f8f6f0 emitted (L6) with cream-math caveat (L187). Palette SoT = HOUSES.palette per L153. No dark tokens. Button + Mandelbrot corners prose at L267-269. Octahedron face order documented prose-only at L277. Mobile-first explicit. Motion as prose only.

Factual spot-checks:
- Plan said `accent: #e3dfd6` and `destructive: #ef4444`; DESIGN.md emits `#e5e2dc` and `#ef4343`. RECOMPUTED HSL(40 15% 88%)→#e5e2dc and HSL(0 84% 60%)→#ef4343 — DESIGN.md is correct, plan was off by rounding. Impl agent caught this.
- All 12 house hexes match HEAD exactly.
- Spacing inventory honest — every declared number is observed in src/ grep (verified: gap, py, space-y, space-x usage).
- Inter declared, prose flags as "not yet implemented" (3 sites).
- No `#1a1a1a`, `#6B4C9A`, `house-publications-accent`, `brand`, `text-default`, `charcoal-deep`, `.dark` tokens declared — all only appear as drift/anti-pattern mentions.

PRD check (FRAC-22): mobile-first 375px constraint is the load-bearing PRD inheritance and is honored 3x. Hogwarts-six-houses framing honored. The PRD's draft palette (#fdf0d5/#cc2936/#1d3557) and font stack (Space Grotesk / Instrument Serif) reflect the March 2026 draft state; DESIGN.md reflects shipped reality which has diverged through FRAC-24/26/27 etc. This is the correct read — DESIGN.md is the SoT for what shipped, not a re-derivation of the early PRD.

No inline fixes made. No source files modified. The document is well-structured, internally consistent, honest about divergence from shipped code, and lints cleanly.

Concerns / open items (informational, not blocking):
- Lint did not fire `missing-primary` — DESIGN.md handles this gracefully at L343 by noting the divergence. The plan's expectation here was incorrect; the implementation correctly documents the actual lint behavior.
- Description spans 2 lines of YAML but is technically one string — valid YAML, lint accepts.

PR-ready.
